overview.short: Talgad fort is located 18 km south of the Roha town on Roha-Tala-Indapur road.
overview.description: Talgad fort is located 18 km south of the Roha town on Roha-Tala-Indapur road. This fort is at an altitude of 1000 feet. This fort is in form of a narrow strip 20 meters in width. This fort is situated on a narrow spur guarded by fortification.

topPlaces.name: Kuda Caves
topPlaces.description: Kuda Caves are located in the small village of Kuda, on the eastern side of the north shore of Murud-Janjira in south Konkan, India. These fifteen Buddhist caves are small, simple, and were excavated in first century BCE. The verandah of Chaitya has several reliefs of the Buddha, carved with symbols of lotus, wheel and Nagas.

items.note: Kuda Caves are located in the small village of Kuda, on the eastern side of the north shore of Murud-Janjira in south Konkan, India. These fifteen Buddhist caves are small, simple, and were excavated in first century BCE. The verandah of Chaitya has several reliefs of the Buddha, carved with symbols of lotus, wheel and Nagas.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Talagad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Talagad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Pune Airport (89 km) and the nearest railway station is Mumbai CSMT (79 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Talagad?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Talagad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Talagad?
faq.a: Stays in Talagad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Talagad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kuda Caves.
